edges.description: Spectrin, protein 4.1R, and related membrane-skeleton defects weaken the horizontal cytoskeletal network underlying the erythrocyte membrane.
edges.source: Red Cell Cytoskeleton Protein Defects

edges.description: Horizontal cytoskeletal weakness reduces mechanical stability and red-cell deformability.
edges.source: Weakened Horizontal Cytoskeletal Associations

edges.description: Reduced membrane stability contributes to elliptocytic and poikilocytic red-cell morphology.
edges.source: Reduced Erythrocyte Deformability and Stability
edges.target: Elliptocytic and Poikilocytic Red Cell Morphology

edges.description: Decreased deformability and stability shorten erythrocyte survival and produce hemolysis in symptomatic cases.
edges.source: Reduced Erythrocyte Deformability and Stability
edges.target: Shortened Red Cell Survival and Hemolysis

nodes.description: Structural erythrocyte abnormalities are an obligate feature of the disorder, with elliptocytes and other abnormal forms detected on blood smear or related laboratory assessment.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001877 | Abnormal erythrocyte morphology | Obligate (100%)

nodes.description: Pigment gallstones may occur as a rare complication of chronic hemolysis and bilirubin turnover.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001081 | Cholelithiasis | Very rare (<4-1%)

nodes.description: Severe forms can present in infancy or the neonatal period with congenital hemolysis, particularly when biallelic or modifying alleles produce hereditary pyropoikilocytosis-like severity.

evidence.snippet: HP:0004804 | Congenital hemolytic anemia | Occasional (29-5%)

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype correlations in hereditary elliptocytosis and hereditary pyropoikilocytosis.
evidence.snippet: HPP is typically diagnosed in patients with family history of HE but presents with increased severity, frequently with transfusion requirement in infancy or, rarely, long-term transfusion-dependence.
EPB41 | erythrocyte membrane protein band 4.1 | hgnc:3377 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) in

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype correlations in hereditary elliptocytosis and hereditary pyropoikilocytosis.
evidence.snippet: All 15 patients were found to have mutations in SPTA1, SPTB or EPB41

nodes.description: Destabilized red-cell membrane architecture produces elliptical erythrocytes and, in more severe forms, poikilocytosis and red-cell fragmentation on peripheral smear.
nodes.id: Elliptocytic and Poikilocytic Red Cell Morphology

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype correlations in hereditary elliptocytosis and hereditary pyropoikilocytosis.
evidence.snippet: The clinical diagnosis of HE and HPP relies on identifying abnormal RBC morphology on peripheral blood smear (elliptocytes, poikilocytosis and fragmented RBCs)

nodes.description: Elliptical or oval erythrocytes on peripheral smear, the defining red-cell morphology for hereditary elliptocytosis.

evidence.snippet: HP:0004445 | Elliptocytosis | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype correlations in hereditary elliptocytosis and hereditary pyropoikilocytosis.
evidence.snippet: Elliptocytes were noted on blood smear and HE was suspected.

nodes.description: Exercise intolerance is an occasional symptom in hereditary elliptocytosis, likely reflecting reduced oxygen delivery during symptomatic anemia or hemolysis.

evidence.snippet: HP:0003546 | Exercise intolerance | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Fatigue can occur in symptomatic individuals with anemia or compensated hemolysis.

evidence.snippet: HP:0012378 | Fatigue | Occasional (29-5%)
GYPC | glycophorin C (Gerbich blood group) | hgnc:4704 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) in

evidence.reference_title: Molecular genetics of hereditary elliptocytosis and hereditary spherocytosis.
evidence.snippet: HE stems from changes in the SPTA1, SPTB, EL1 and (exceptionally) GPYC genes that encode spectrin alpha- and beta- chains, protein 4.1 and glycophorin C/D, respectively.

nodes.description: Variable hemolytic anemia may occur, from mild compensated hemolysis to severe transfusion-dependent disease in severe or biallelic presentations.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001878 | Hemolytic anemia | Occasional (29-5%)

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype correlations in hereditary elliptocytosis and hereditary pyropoikilocytosis.
evidence.snippet: Patient # 1 (Figure 2) was a 12-month-old Caucasian boy who had presented with mild hemolytic anemia at 4 months of age

nodes.description: Severe fetal or neonatal hereditary elliptocytosis-spectrum presentations can rarely include hydrops fetalis.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001789 | Hydrops fetalis | Very rare (<4-1%)

nodes.description: Hemolysis can elevate bilirubin outside the neonatal period, contributing to jaundice and gallstone risk.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002904 | Hyperbilirubinemia | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Abnormal osmotic fragility or osmotic-gradient ektacytometry reflects impaired red-cell membrane biomechanical properties.

evidence.snippet: HP:0005502 | Increased red cell osmotic fragility | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype correlations in hereditary elliptocytosis and hereditary pyropoikilocytosis.
evidence.snippet: The resulting ektacytometry curve reflects biomechanical properties of the RBCs including osmotic fragility, surface-to-volume ratio, cytoskeleton flexibility, and cytoplasmic viscosity.

nodes.description: Jaundice can accompany bilirubin elevation from hemolysis.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000952 | Jaundice | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Neonatal hyperbilirubinemia can occur in infants with hereditary elliptocytosis or more severe HE/HPP-spectrum disease.

evidence.snippet: HP:0003265 | Neonatal hyperbilirubinemia | Occasional (29-5%)

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype correlations in hereditary elliptocytosis and hereditary pyropoikilocytosis.
evidence.snippet: Both patients with the missense mutation p.D791E were asymptomatic at baseline but had ektacytometry profile consistent with mild HE; one presented with neonatal hyperbilirubinemia

nodes.description: Abnormally shaped erythrocytes beyond elliptocytes can occur, especially in severe hereditary pyropoikilocytosis-spectrum presentations.

evidence.snippet: HP:0004447 | Poikilocytosis | Occasional (29-5%)

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype correlations in hereditary elliptocytosis and hereditary pyropoikilocytosis.
evidence.snippet: Peripheral blood smears demonstrate increased poikilocytosis and RBC fragmentation compared to HE.

nodes.description: Prolonged neonatal jaundice can occur in infants with hereditary elliptocytosis-spectrum hemolysis.

evidence.snippet: HP:0006579 | Prolonged neonatal jaundice | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Red-cell transfusion is supportive therapy for severe congenital or transfusion-dependent hemolytic anemia in the severe HE/HPP spectrum.

evidence.snippet: manifestations ranging from mild to severe transfusion-dependent hemolytic anemia

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype correlations in hereditary elliptocytosis and hereditary pyropoikilocytosis.
evidence.snippet: HPP is typically diagnosed in patients with family history of HE but presents with increased severity, frequently with transfusion requirement in infancy or, rarely, long-term transfusion-dependence.

nodes.description: Pathogenic variants in erythrocyte membrane-skeleton genes affect spectrin, protein 4.1R, or glycophorin C components that maintain red cell membrane shape and mechanical resilience.

evidence.snippet: SPTA1 | spectrin alpha, erythrocytic 1 | hgnc:11272 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) in
SPTB | spectrin beta, erythrocytic | hgnc:11274 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) in

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary elliptocytosis: clinical, morphological and biochemical studies of 38 cases.
evidence.snippet: The major determinant of membrane shape and stability is a proteinaceous meshwork named membrane skeleton, composed mainly of spectrin, actin, protein 4.1 and ankyrin.

nodes.description: Defective cytoskeletal mechanics decrease erythrocyte deformability and mechanical stability, reducing red-cell survival and producing abnormal osmotic-gradient ektacytometry profiles.

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary elliptocytosis: clinical, morphological and biochemical studies of 38 cases.
evidence.snippet: A study of red cell deformability using an ektacytometer revealed that the cell deformability under isotonic conditions was decreased in all HE patients.

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype correlations in hereditary elliptocytosis and hereditary pyropoikilocytosis.
evidence.snippet: The typical HE ektacytometry curve is trapezoidal with decreased RBC deformability

nodes.description: Increased reticulocytes can accompany compensated hemolysis as marrow output rises to replace shortened-survival erythrocytes.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001923 | Reticulocytosis | Occasional (29-5%)
SPTA1 | spectrin alpha, erythrocytic 1 | hgnc:11272 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) in

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype correlations in hereditary elliptocytosis and hereditary pyropoikilocytosis.
evidence.snippet: Ten patients had mutations in SPTA1 only
SPTB | spectrin beta, erythrocytic | hgnc:11274 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) in

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype correlations in hereditary elliptocytosis and hereditary pyropoikilocytosis.
evidence.snippet: one in SPTB, one was compound heterozygous for mutations affecting both α- and β-spectrin and one had a mutation in EPB41.

nodes.description: Reduced membrane stability and deformability shorten erythrocyte survival. This drives variable hemolysis, ranging from compensated or asymptomatic disease to severe transfusion-dependent anemia.
nodes.id: Shortened Red Cell Survival and Hemolysis

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype correlations in hereditary elliptocytosis and hereditary pyropoikilocytosis.
evidence.snippet: The resulting defects alter the horizontal cytoskeletal associations and affect RBC membrane stability and deformability causing shortened RBC survival.

evidence.reference_title: Inherited hemolytic anemia: a possessive beginner's guide.
evidence.snippet: Membrane structural defects lead to hereditary spherocytosis (HS) and hereditary elliptocytosis (HE)

nodes.description: Skin ulceration is an occasional complication reported in the Orphanet phenotype record for hereditary elliptocytosis.

evidence.snippet: HP:0200042 | Skin ulcer | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Splenectomy can ameliorate anemia in selected symptomatic patients with red-cell membrane structural disorders by increasing circulating red-cell lifespan; patient selection must consider severity and long-term risks.

evidence.reference_title: Inherited hemolytic anemia: a possessive beginner's guide.
evidence.snippet: splenectomy leads to amelioration of anemia by increasing the circulatory red cell life span.

nodes.description: Splenic enlargement can occur with chronic hemolysis and erythrocyte clearance in symptomatic patients.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001744 | Splenomegaly | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Stomatocytic red cells can be present in a subset, especially overlapping ovalocytosis or stomatocytic elliptocytosis presentations.

evidence.snippet: HP:0004446 | Stomatocytosis | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Mutations affecting spectrin self-association or protein 4.1R-mediated junctional complexes weaken horizontal links in the erythrocyte cytoskeleton.

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype correlations in hereditary elliptocytosis and hereditary pyropoikilocytosis.
evidence.snippet: The resulting defects alter the horizontal cytoskeletal associations and affect RBC membrane stability and deformability causing shortened RBC survival.

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary elliptocytosis: clinical, morphological and biochemical studies of 38 cases.
evidence.snippet: We observed two types of membrane defects in the 38 patients studied: 24 patients (13 kindreds) exhibited spectrin self-association defect (type I HE) and 14 patients (6 kindreds) displayed deficiency in protein 4.1.

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary pyropoikilocytosis and elliptocytosis in a Caucasian family. Transmission of the same molecular defect in spectrin through three generations with different clinical expression.
evidence.snippet: Our studies revealed a defective ability of spectrin to self-associate
